title: lepidotheca santolina
intro: Lepidotheca santolina, also known as Santolina or Cotton Lavender, is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its silvery-gray foliage and small yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which make it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Lepidotheca santolina include its ability to soothe digestive problems, reduce anxiety and stress, and aid in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Lepidotheca santolina can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or essential oil, and is also used in herbal remedies, topical creams, and perfumes.
